user_query:
I need a quick team-ready security update based on our dependency scan. Please do the following in this workspace:

1) Run the dependency vulnerability validation using the provided tool and inputs, saving results to the out directory:
   - Command to run:
     python3 tools/deps_audit.py --deps input/dependencies.json --db input/vuln_db.json --out out/scan_results.json

2) Compose a concise status update for the engineering team at out/security_status_update.md that includes:
   - A summary of the new scan (out/scan_results.json): total vulnerabilities and a severity breakdown (High, Medium, Low) exactly as recorded in the JSON.
   - A comparison against the baseline (input/baseline_scan.json):
     • "New since baseline": list each vulnerability present in out/scan_results.json but not in input/baseline_scan.json, keyed by (package, version, id). Format each as: - {id} on {package}@{version} ({severity}).
     • "Resolved since baseline": list each vulnerability present in input/baseline_scan.json but not in out/scan_results.json in the same format.
   - A short "Next actions" section with one line for every High severity issue in the current scan: Action: prioritize patch for {package}@{version} (ID: {id}).
   - A closing line noting the validation command you ran and the output path of the scan results.

Inputs:
- input/dependencies.json, input/vuln_db.json (validation scan inputs)
- input/baseline_scan.json (previous run for comparison)

Outputs to create:
- out/scan_results.json (machine-readable scan output generated by the validation command)
- out/security_status_update.md (human-readable team status update based on the results and baseline)

Notes:
- Use only the provided inputs and tool. Do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- The report should reflect the exact counts from out/scan_results.json and the difference vs input/baseline_scan.json.
- Ensure the out directory exists and the specified files are created. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
